shape_semantics: cmp=W, vp=L, vpc=_, naive=L -> cmplog (I2S) resolves; value_profile AND naive block; value_profile_cmplog non-decisive. i2s-PRO where the value_profile GRADIENT does NOT help: the resolving move is exact-literal substitution / derived-operand construction / structural reach, not Hamming-gradient climbing. The G1 discriminator vs the vp-gradient families is that value_profile, with full CMP_MAP feedback, still BLOCKS here.

hypotheses.label: cmplog plants an exact fixed-offset literal/magic constant the VP gradient cannot climb to

params.note: fixed-offset VALUE gate; target byte(s) = the intercepted literal at the gate offset, decoy = a blocking-seed value at the same offset

per_branch_gate.curl_152: ASCII 'Content-Type:' literal (43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 2d 54 79 70 65 3a) at the header offset
per_branch_gate.harfbuzz_5494: format field in {1,2,3} plus 'MATH' FOURCC tag

hypotheses.prediction: cmplog's saved corpus is enriched in the TARGET literal byte(s) at the gate offset relative to naive (signed_target_enrich > 0), because I2SRandReplace plants the intercepted constant. A pure value_profile gradient mechanism would instead drive the byte toward the target by Hamming distance and would show no cmplog-specific literal enrichment; the shape already records that value_profile BLOCKS (it is an L), so the literal is NOT gradient-reachable. This rules out the vp-gradient alternative: if the operand were gradient-climbable, value_profile would resolve (be a W) and naive's corpus would drift toward the target rather than staying at decoy values.
hypotheses.g2_independence: operand_enrichment compares cmplog-vs-naive CORPUS byte composition at the gate offset; it does not consult the vpc/vp resolve pattern that defined the shape, so it is independent of the label source.

hypotheses.label: cmplog constructs a DERIVED negative/range value across parser-routing bytes (no stored literal)

params.note: NO fixed literal: operand_literal=null, operand_width_bytes=null, byte_signature=unclear. The winning value is a derived signed integer (%d arg < 0) assembled from multi-field tag/colon/path/numeric bytes, NOT a single intercepted constant at a fixed offset.

hypotheses.prediction: operand_enrichment finds NO fixed-offset target signature for these branches and returns no_gate_signature (per its scope_limits: it skips gates with no fixed literal). The ABSENCE of a clean per-offset literal enrichment, combined with the same i2s-pro shape (cmplog W, vp+naive L), distinguishes derived-operand construction from exact-literal substitution: a stored-literal mechanism would yield a positive signed_target_enrich at a fixed offset, which this branch lacks.
hypotheses.rule: no_gate_signature == true (operand_enrichment returns no fixed-offset literal); hypothesis confirmed by the diagnostic ABSENCE of literal enrichment under the i2s-pro shape, falling to inconclusive if any clean fixed-offset literal IS found (would reclassify to i2s_exact_literal_constant_gate)
hypotheses.g2_independence: the no_gate_signature determination is a property of the gate's byte structure (whether a fixed-offset target literal exists), independent of the resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: cmplog's resolving corpus drives the upstream table-directory/ScriptList PARSE deep enough to reach and re-enter the script-lookup branch per seed; the blocking corpus aborts the parse early (a structural REACH gate, not a single comparable literal)

params.note: Run cmplog-RESOLVING (W) vs naive-BLOCKING (L) seeds through libafl-harfbuzz-cov; read line 573 (find_script_index) per-seed-normalized execution count. The mechanism is STRUCTURAL REACH: a well-formed sfnt blob whose table directory + GPOS/GSUB ScriptList successfully parse drives the script-lookup loop far more per seed than the 6-64 byte near-random naive fragments, whose malformed header aborts the parse before the ScriptList is ever populated. This is NOT a single fixed-offset literal (operand_enrichment scored rule_not_met) NOR a size+token-count assembly contrast (joint_necessity's size_lift/cmp_tags scored rule_not_met because the resolving seeds carry the structure as parse-derived offsets, not as enough literal table/script tag tokens to count) -- it is the per-seed execution DEPTH of the branch line itself.

hypotheses.prediction: The cmplog-resolving seeds execute the find_script_index branch line markedly MORE per seed than the naive-blocking seeds (depth_lift >> 1, winner_deeper == true), because their successful sfnt/table parse reaches and iterates the script lookup while the malformed blocking fragments never get there. This is the discriminating signal that separates a STRUCTURAL-REACH gate from (a) the single fixed-offset exact-literal hypothesis -- which predicts equal reach with both arms hitting the line and differing in one byte window (depth_lift ~ 1, the test that DID hold for harfbuzz_5483/5494 under operand_enrichment) -- and from (b) the already-refuted size+token assembly story (joint_necessity rule_not_met: not enough countable literal tags), since reach is measured by per-seed line-execution count, not seed size or token count. It also rules out the vp-gradient alternative: value_profile (an L) cannot Hamming-climb a malformed fragment into a parse that reaches the deep script-lookup; if the gate were a single climbable operand on a path both arms already reach, the shape would not show vp=L.
hypotheses.g2_independence: depth_reach measures the per-seed line-execution COUNT each arm's CORPUS drives at the branch line (structural reach), using depth_lift (line-execution count) NOT side_lift (which-side-taken). It never consults the vp/vpc resolve pattern that defined the shape; the cmplog-vs-naive reach contrast is the campaign-data signal, independent of the analysis-derived label source.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage 'I2S event' descriptor proposed (I2S is untagged in mutation_op) -- the discriminators are corpus byte-composition enrichment, gate byte-structure, and per-seed structural reach depth, not lineage events.

No value_profile 'gradient event' descriptor proposed (VP is an invisible CMP_MAP feedback) -- the claim that VP does NOT help rests on the shape's own vp=L plus the operand_kind/reach structure, not on a measured gradient event.

harfbuzz_5562 is a structural gate with different-SIZED resolving vs blocking seeds, so byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is the WRONG test (it scored rule_not_met). The PRIOR round-1 pivot to joint_necessity's struct_size_and_token_count ALSO scored rule_not_met (the resolving seeds carry structure as parse-derived table-directory offsets, with too few literal table/script-tag tokens to satisfy cmp_tags>=2 -- consistent with the analysis weakest_evidence_point that the 'dflt' tag bytes are not directly visible in the resolving seeds). This round pivots to a genuinely DIFFERENT, not-yet-tried tool (depth_reach) and a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism (per-seed structural reach of the branch line), NOT a relaxed-threshold re-run of either refuted test.

depth_reach runs through libafl-harfbuzz-cov (on-disk + cov-image present on s4) and needs >=1 on-disk W + >=1 on-disk L seed; harfbuzz is on-disk on s4 with 3 cmplog resolving + 20 naive blocking seeds, so harfbuzz_5562 is scorable. operand_enrichment needs an on-disk corpus + >=3 W + >=3 L: SCORABLE only for curl + harfbuzz; bloaty_34 has NO on-disk corpus (bloaty not in {curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3}) so it CANNOT be enrichment-scored and carries to fallback inconclusive on the measurement, even though its mechanism clearly matches the literal hypothesis.
notes: ROUND-2 re-design (loop-until-dry). PRIORS KEPT (verbatim, superset/monotonic): (1) i2s_exact_literal_constant_gate, (2) i2s_derived_signed_operand_construction -- both retained so the 6 confirmed branches re-validate. The round-1 NEW hypothesis i2s_multiliteral_structural_assembly_gate (joint_necessity / size_lift>=1.3 AND cmp_tags>=2) was tested on the sole unlabeled branch harfbuzz_5562 and scored rule_not_met (campaign data did not support the size+token-assembly mechanism) -- per G1 it is NOT re-proposed and NOT re-tested at a relaxed threshold; it is REPLACED. NEW MECHANISM (1): i2s_structural_reach_gate for harfbuzz_5562 -- a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism: not exact-literal substitution (operand_enrichment rule_not_met) and not size/token assembly (joint_necessity rule_not_met), but the per-seed structural REACH of the script-lookup branch line: the cmplog-resolving corpus is well-formed sfnt that parses deep enough to reach/iterate find_script_index, while the naive-blocking corpus aborts the parse early. Measured by REUSING the BUILT depth_reach / depth_reach descriptor (per-seed branch-line execution count, cmplog-W vs naive-L arms) -- one of the registry tools NOT yet tried on this shape (pivot per the round-2 brief: value_distance_reached/depth_reach/corpus_size_ratio/token_count). G1 novelty: discriminates structural reach (depth_lift>>1) from single-literal substitution (depth_lift~1, both arms reach the line, byte_freq_ratio is the right test) AND from size+token assembly (already refuted; reach is line-execution count, not seed size/token count) AND from the vp-gradient alternative (VP=L cannot Hamming-climb a malformed fragment into a deep parse). Uses depth_lift + winner_deeper, deliberately NOT side_lift (side_lift = which-side-taken, closer to re-deriving the resolve label -> a G2 risk). NO decidable:false sub-group this round -- harfbuzz_5562 is on-disk + cov-imaged on s4 and its reach mechanism IS discriminable by depth_reach, so an honest inconclusive is not yet warranted; if depth_lift comes back ~1 (both arms reach the line equally), the rule correctly leaves the branch at fallback inconclusive rather than forcing a label. CAVEAT (carried): the 'vp does not help' claim is asserted from the shape (vp=L) + operand_kind/reach structure, NOT independently measured (VP is an unobservable feedback); this is the honest discrimination ceiling for the WL_L shape and is why the cmplog-vs-naive reach contrast (not a VP contrast) is the load-bearing test.
